decisions.title_en: Anduril raises $5B Series H at $61B valuation led by Thrive + a16z
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. Defense-AI funding milestone: $5B Series H doubles valuation to $61B in 11 months; led by Thrive Capital + a16z; revenue doubled to $2.2B in 2025; Anduril positioned as central winner of Trump's $185B Golden Dome missile defense system.

decisions.title_en: Cerebras begins Nasdaq trading May 14 as largest 2026 tech IPO
decisions.reason: Previously published 2026-05-12 about pricing upsize. New development today: actual Nasdaq trading debut under ticker CBRS at $150-160/share, $48.8B valuation, 20x oversubscribed. Polymarket implies 94% probability of $50B+ closing market cap. Substantively new info: actual market reception vs. prior pre-trading.

decisions.title_en: Trump arrives Beijing for Xi summit; Jensen Huang added last-minute by Trump call
decisions.reason: Previously published 2026-05-12 noting Huang's conspicuous absence from delegation. New development today: Trump personally called Huang after media coverage, Huang flew to Alaska to board Air Force One, full reversal. Also: Trump arrived Beijing 5-13 (vs. prior anticipated arrival). Substantively new and noteworthy political-tech moment.

decisions.title_en: Altman day 2 + Microsoft Wetter discloses $100B+ total OpenAI spend
decisions.reason: Previously published 2026-05-12 about Altman's first day of testimony (90% equity, etc.). New developments 5-13: (1) Microsoft corp-dev exec Michael Wetter discloses Microsoft has spent $100B+ on OpenAI through June 2026 including infrastructure + hosting + investments; (2) Altman cross-examination second day with new 'OpenAI nonprofit was left for dead' framing of Musk's abandonment; (3) Sutskever testimony reveal of $7B equity stake + 2023 'pattern of lying' memo. Substantively new financial disclosure and testimony.

decisions.title_en: Anthropic $1.5B Bartz copyright settlement final approval hearing today
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Today (5-14, 2pm PT) is final approval/fairness hearing for the largest copyright settlement in US history ($1.5B). 91.3% of eligible works (440,490 of 482,460) already claimed; ~$3,000/title minimum payout. Sets precedent for entire AI industry on training-data fair use vs. pirated library liability.

decisions.title_en: Microsoft shopping AI startups for life after OpenAI; in talks with Inception
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Reuters exclusive 5-13: Microsoft preparing for future independent of OpenAI by shopping for AI startups; in advanced talks with Stanford-spun-out Inception (>$1B price); previously backed away from Cursor over GitHub Copilot antitrust concerns. Material strategic shift signaling post-OpenAI Microsoft AI roadmap.

decisions.title_en: Google GTIG thwarts first-ever AI-built zero-day mass exploitation campaign
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Google Threat Intelligence Group 5-11 announcement: high-confidence first observed real-world case of cybercriminals using an AI model to discover and weaponize a zero-day vulnerability (2FA bypass in popular open-source web admin platform) for planned mass exploitation campaign. John Hultquist: 'It's here.' Neither Gemini nor Mythos involved per Google. Independent threshold event for AI cybersecurity.

decisions.title_en: Android Show I/O Edition day 2: Gemini Intelligence as system-level agent across Android, app automation, Auto
decisions.reason: Previously published 2026-05-12 about Googlebook laptop reveal. New 5-13 announcements at same show but covering broader Android platform: Gemini Intelligence positioned as cross-app operating layer (not just laptop), app automation rolling to Pixel/Samsung this summer, Android Auto biggest maps update in a decade, smarter Chrome on Android, sweeping security features. Substantively new system-level rollout vs. prior laptop-focused angle.

decisions.title_en: SMIC wins approval for $5.98B SMIC North buyout — China's largest wafer-foundry M&A
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. SMIC 5-12 received regulatory approval for ¥40.6B (~$5.98B) share-issuance acquisition of remaining 49% of SMIC North (Beijing 12-inch mature-node fab) from Big Fund Phase I + Beijing IC Fund. Largest domestic wafer-foundry M&A in China history; consolidates state resources around national champion under US export controls. AI-infrastructure self-sufficiency angle.

decisions.title_en: White House AI pre-release vetting EO stalled by internal infighting — Wiles/Sacks walk back Hassett's 'FDA for AI'
decisions.reason: Previously published 2026-05-06 (Hassett 'FDA-style' EO predicted in two weeks) and 5-5 (CAISI voluntary agreements). New 5-13 development: chief of staff Susie Wiles and former AI czar David Sacks publicly walked back Hassett's FDA-for-AI framing; EO stalled by internal disagreement; administration may wait on China summit outcome. Substantively new political development reversing prior timeline.
